title: LCL Why do certain portions of the body not go
display_title: LCL Why do certain portions of the body not go
wikitext:
[]: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by SweetAngel » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:09 pm I hope I'm not making a redundant thread, I've seen threads discussing why their clothes didn't go (they're dead dead things and/or they don't have a recognizable form to the animals/plants that they consisted of), whether plants or animals can go (a tentative yes from what I could gather), but I want to know why some portions of people's body gets tangified and why they vary between person. Like why does one person (i.e. Maya) lose their hand while others lose their spine while others don't lose any body parts/components upon tangificiation? If they were to reform, would it be without the untangified portions of their body? Its clear LCL can't regenerate body parts that were already missing (Asuka still had her injuries), but why did they lose the body parts in the first place and could it regenerate those? This always bugged me, thank you for any answers.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:11 pm Dramatic effect.

[]:             Quote Post by SweetAngel » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:22 pm Well, everything happens for drama/psychological effect on the audience and/or characters but its usually self-consistent (like, Evas may not follow laws of gravity by not collapsing under their own weight, but they generally follow their own rules on how to create and pilot them) but there is no self-consistency maintained in tangificiation even in the same scene, so I figured there was a justification given for this elsewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:37 pm Everyone "popped" when they turned except for Maya, her hand did the effect to show what Lilith/Ritsuko typed on the computer. As for Keel, he was part cyborg so the non organic components didn't get tanged.

[]: Re: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:46 pm SweetAngel wrote: Like why does one person (i.e. Maya) lose their hand while others lose their spine while others don't lose any body parts/components upon tangificiation?

[]: Re: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by SweetAngel » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:56 pm Azathoth wrote: Maya's hand pops off while it's still coherent because that makes for a better shot. Kiel's spine doesn't dissolve because it's synthetic, being that he's a cyborg.

[]: Re: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 09, 2010 3:15 pm SweetAngel wrote: Ah, okay, that makes sense, about Kiel, but still, I think the same shot could've been accomplished by the hand popping off, hitting the monitor, before dissolving into a puddle, but the hand keeps intact, or maybe it dissolves later?

[]: Re: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Oct 09, 2010 3:21 pm Azathoth wrote: Do you have a screenshot? I can't recall.

[]:             Quote Post by SweetAngel » Sat Oct 09, 2010 3:29 pm ^ Yep thats the one! Do you have a screenshot? I can't recall.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Oct 09, 2010 4:34 pm SweetAngel wrote: And immediately after that shot we see fully tanged people. Why?

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Oct 09, 2010 4:41 pm Why is that right hand of his left untanged? Because it was Touma. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by beaverteeth92 » Mon Oct 11, 2010 11:33 am Because it looks cool. Don't overthink it.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 11, 2010 12:21 pm Clearly the man has a prosthetic. He's very self-conscious about it. Way to keep bringing it up you assholes.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Oct 11, 2010 6:46 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: Dramatic effect.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Oct 12, 2010 6:51 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Clearly the man has a prosthetic

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Oct 12, 2010 9:25 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: This could be true, but I don´t think that soldiers are allowed to have artificial body parts.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Oct 12, 2010 9:29 am ^ Yeah, I figured that it would be kind of hard to recruit enough soldiers after the second impact. ... WAIT. What if that unlucky guy LOST HIS ARM IN THE SECOND IMPACT. Think about it, this gives the shot a whole new meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Oct 17, 2010 5:58 am Perhaps that Dude's arm (as Maya's Hand and the rest of Gendo) tangified a bit later. When we get the panorama shot of that corridor, the Arm isn't there anymore. It probably just is that way. ppl don't always tangify symethrically. It wasn't stated anywhere that all of the body has to liquify at the same Moment. The Editors probably did this to a) get some Hand shots in there (Anno luvz Hands) b) teh creep factor and c) for Gendo's remains to be still recognizable as Gendo and to creep us out with his freakin' spine.

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Sun Oct 17, 2010 1:44 pm maya's hand hit the screen as it was dissolving and soldier's hand melted after the blood did. as you saw, all the corpses melted away leaving no detached body parts. maya's hand did that to bring attention to what was written on the screen in a creative manner and the soldier's hand was just a good spot to show all the blood reverting into primordial fluids.

[]: Re: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by Aozora » Thu Oct 21, 2010 1:23 pm Azathoth wrote: Asuka's injuries involved her arm being split in half down the middle all the way to the shoulder (not possible but whatever), her eye being punctured (actually, at a guess, I'd say the whole left side of her face gets generally screwed up, since the eye itself wouldn't bleed so much), and her entire body getting penetrated by eight (or nine? I always forget the number) two-pronged spears. If these injuries hadn't regenerated she would be dead. The bandages are there because she wanted them to be there, not because she's actually injured.
summary: []: LCL (Why do certain portions of the body not go?)             Quote Post by SweetAngel » Sat Oct 09, 2010 2:09 pm I hope I'm not making a redundant thread, I've seen threads discussing why their clothes didn't go (they're dead dead things and/or they don't have a recognizable form to the animals/plants that they consisted of), whether plants or animals can go (a tentative yes from what I could gather)
